pr.title: Improve item, ftCommon, and gmmain_lib matches
pr.author: dberweger2017

summary: PR #2754 made source-shape adjustments in ftCommon, gmmain_lib, and Pikachu Thunder item code to improve MWCC matching without intended behavior changes. The PR body reports full matches for it_802B1DF8 and ftCo_800B1478, and bot output reports three new matches plus five unmatched-function improvements.

key_files.role: Adjusted ftCommon function source shapes for ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2C80, ftCo_800AF78C, and ftCo_800B1478; ftCo_800B1478 became a reported full match.
Adjusted gmmain_lib source shape, stack padding, declaration placement, direct data access, and indexing in gmMainLib_8015EA80, gmMainLib_8015F150, and gmMainLib_8015F600.
Adjusted it_802B1DF8 initialization and loop header shape; PR notes identify unusual assignment shape for MWCC zero-register reuse, and the function became a reported full match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Notes and Matching sections for it_802B1DF8

what_changed: it_802B1DF8 changed initialization order from separate cur_delay and spawn.x3C_damage zeroing to spawn.x3C_damage = (cur_delay = 0), followed by a second cur_delay = 0, and moved first = NULL into the for-loop initializer.
ftCo_800A229C reduced PAD_STACK from 8 to 4, changed some 0.0f comparisons to 0.0, and replaced a manual absolute-value branch for sp20.y with ABS(sp20.y).
ftCo_800A2C80 removed a local Fighter_x1A88_t* data variable and directly accessed fp->x1A88.xFA_b6 and fp->x1A88.xFA_b5.
ftCo_800AF78C introduced an item_data pointer, combined target_slot assignment with the x44 store, and used item_data for x4C updates.
ftCo_800B1478 removed a separate target_slot setup statement and used *(target_slot = &fp->x1A88.x44) = target.
gmMainLib_8015EA80 changed a typed VsModeData* base access into char* base pointer arithmetic with explicit byte offsets for player handicap stores.

gmMainLib_8015F150 added PAD_STACK(8), changed from GetPersistentFighterData((u8)i) to direct gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.x1F2C base indexing, and passed an address derived from the direct x1F2C path to gmMainLib_8015EF30.
gmMainLib_8015F600 reduced PAD_STACK from 104 to 96, moved j and bank_offset declarations into branch-local scopes, and reloaded bank/data before copying name-tag text.

smart_moves.text: The PR states no behavior change was intended and frames the edits as source-shape adjustments for MWCC register allocation and scheduling, helping reviewers interpret unusual C shapes as matching tactics rather than logic changes.
The author recorded verifier commands, including diff check, targeted ninja object builds, and objdiff-cli checks for the listed functions.
Replacing a manual absolute-value branch with ABS(sp20.y) in ftCo_800A229C aligns with the loaded canonical-control-flow-and-macros standard when the macro expresses the source operation.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small expression-shape changes can matter for MWCC register allocation: in it_802B1DF8, the PR notes that assigning spawn.x3C_damage through (cur_delay = 0) was needed so MWCC reused the target zero register for both the s16 spawn damage store and the loop-carried delay value.

Declaration placement and initialization placement can affect matching: gmMainLib_8015F600 moved j and bank_offset into narrower branch-local scopes, and it_802B1DF8 moved first = NULL into the for-loop initializer.

Pointer alias and access-path choices can perturb MWCC scheduling/register allocation: the PR changes between direct field access, saved pointers, combined pointer assignment/store expressions, and direct global data paths across ftCo_0A01 and gmmain_lib while reporting match improvements.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800AF78C item_data and target_slot expression

naming_conventions.name: item_data
naming_conventions.context: New local pointer in ftCo_800AF78C for &fp->x1A88 used on item-related x4C updates.

naming_conventions.name: base
naming_conventions.context: Used as a local base pointer in both gmMainLib_8015EA80 and gmMainLib_8015F150, but with different types and access styles.

naming_conventions.name: target_slot
naming_conventions.context: Used as a Fighter** local whose assignment was combined with the target store in ftCo_800AF78C and ftCo_800B1478.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a compound assignment inside a store to influence zero-register reuse.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Unusual source shape; should remain narrowly justified by objdiff evidence under matching-tactics-need-evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Tune PAD_STACK size to adjust local stack shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.example: PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(104) to PAD_STACK(96), and adding PAD_STACK(8).
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: PAD_STACK is a matching tactic that needs verifier evidence and should not be generalized as style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Change local variable lifetime and declaration scope to influence scheduling/register allocation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.example: Move j and bank_offset declarations into gmMainLib_8015F600 branches; initialize first in it_802B1DF8 for-loop header.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Useful as a matching tactic only when build/objdiff evidence supports it.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Change between typed access, direct global access, and raw byte-offset access to affect codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.example: gmMainLib_8015EA80 changed VsModeData* indexed field access to char* base plus explicit offsets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Raw pointer arithmetic conflicts with the general typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard unless retained as a narrow, evidence-backed matching exception.

notes: The PR body provides explicit matching percentages, intent, a concrete note for it_802B1DF8, and verification commands.
The diff is available and untruncated for all three changed files.
There are no inline review comments explaining reviewer rationale; approval is present but empty.
Reported validation commands and objdiff checks are author-provided PR evidence; this intake did not independently rerun builds or objdiff.
Some retained tactics, especially raw byte-offset pointer arithmetic and PAD_STACK changes, are evidence-backed for this PR but should not be promoted as broad style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: it_802B1DF8 zero-register reuse via compound assignment
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2754, it_802B1DF8 reached a reported 100% match after changing spawn damage initialization to spawn.x3C_damage = (cur_delay = 0); the PR notes this unusual assignment shape made MWCC reuse the target zero register for both the s16 spawn damage store and loop-carried delay value.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR #2754 local lifetime and access-path shaping
accepted_candidate_records.text: Across ftCo_0A01 and gmmain_lib, the PR improved matches by changing local pointer lifetimes, combining assignment with stores, changing declaration scope, and selecting different access paths. Treat these as narrow MWCC matching tactics tied to the listed functions rather than general source style.

source_update_candidates.title: Document compound assignment as a narrow MWCC register-allocation tactic
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2754 provides a concrete example where an unusual compound assignment in it_802B1DF8 was explicitly used to make MWCC reuse a zero register. Consider adding this as an example under matching-tactics-need-evidence, with the warning that it requires function-specific objdiff evidence and should not be normalized as style.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standard already covers unusual matching tactics generally; this PR supplies a specific recurring class of expression-shape tactic that may be useful as an example.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify raw byte-offset access as an exception only when typed access fails matching
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2754 changed gmMainLib_8015EA80 from typed VsModeData field indexing to char* byte-offset indexing to improve matching. This should not weaken typed-fields-over-pointer-math; if documented, frame it as a narrow matching exception requiring objdiff evidence and later cleanup/type recovery review.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR contains a typed-access regression for matching purposes; curator may want a cautionary example rather than an accepted convention.

source_update_candidates.title: gmmain_lib matching may be sensitive to gmMainLib_804D3EE0 access paths and local declaration scope
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2754, gmMainLib_8015F150 and gmMainLib_8015F600 improved after direct gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.x1F2C access, added/reduced PAD_STACK, and branch-local declarations. Consider a path-scoped fact if similar gmmain_lib matching work recurs.
source_update_candidates.reason: The evidence is path-specific and may help future gmmain_lib matching, but it should be curator-reviewed before becoming an accepted path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not promote raw char* pointer arithmetic in gmMainLib_8015EA80 as a general decomp style; it conflicts with typed field access standards and is only evidenced here as a matching tactic.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the empty approval and draft-unmark comment; no inline review rationale was loaded.
Do not treat PAD_STACK size changes as inherently preferred source; the PR evidence supports them only for the listed matching improvements.
